DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, HEARING RE: PLANTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT' COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S MAY 10, 2019 ORDERS AND FOR SANCTIONS. (PER OST ORDER FILED ON 6-18-19). THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED. WHILE THE DISCOVERY RESPONSES ORDERED BY THE MAY 10, 2019, ORDER WERE ORIGINALLY UNVERIFIED, WHICH MAY BE TREATED AS NON-RESPONSIVE, VERIFICATIONS WERE PROVIDED AND I DECLINE TO EXERCISE MY DISCRETION TO SANCTION THE DELAY. THE SANCTIONS ORDERED IN THE MAY 10 ORDER WERE CONDITIONAL: IF DEFENDANT PROVIDED THE ORDERED DISCOVERY BY THE TIME ORDERED, THE SANCTIONS WOULD NOT BE IMPOSED, IF NOT, THE SANCTIONS WERE DUE. THIS WAS INTENDED TO INCENTIVIZE TIMELY COMPLIANCE. OTHER THAN WITH RESPECT TO THE VERIFICATION ISSUE DISCUSSED ABOVE, DEFENDANTS COMPLIED WITH THE ORDER, AND I CONCLUDE THIS WAS SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIANT SUCH THAT THE SANCTIONS WERE NOT TRIGGERED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100068943) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BYRD JR, EDWARD A HEARING SET FOR MAY-10-2019 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-10-2019 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EDWARD BYRD'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES,SET ONE AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS: THE MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED RESPONSES IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE VERIFICATIONS NO LATER THAN MAY 17, 2019. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $60 TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF THE FILING FEE. DEFENDANTS ARGUE THAT SANCTIONS ARE INAPPROPRIATE UNDER CCP 2030.300(D) BECAUSE DEFENDANTS DID NOT OPPOSE THE MOTION AND THEREFORE DID NOT "UNSUCCESSFULLYOPPOSE[] A MOTION TO COMPEL." WHILE THIS IS TRUE FOR PURPOSES OF CCP 2030.300(D), FAILING TO PROVIDE THE VERIFICATIONS IN A TIMELY MANNER, WHICH REQUIRED PLAINTIFF TO FILE THIS MOTION, WAS A FAILURE TO CONFER FOR PURPOSES OF CCP 2023.020. SUCH FAILURE TO MEET AND CONFER IS A "SEPARATE OFFENSE ENTITLING THE MOVING PARTY TO MONETARY SANCTIONS 'NOTWITHSTANDING THE OUTCOME OF THE PARTICULAR DISCOVERY MOTION.'" PARKER V. WOLTERS KLUWER UNITED STATES INC. (2007) 149 CAL.APP.4TH 285 (CITING CCP 2023.020). MOREOVER, AS CITED IN PLAINTIFF'S REPLY, CRC 3.1348(A) PERMITS THE COURT TO AWARD SANCTIONS EVEN IF DISCOVERY IS PROVIDED OR THERE IS NO OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant'S Employee Joel Kellogg And Designated Person Most Knowledgable In Compliance With Notice Of Deposition And Production Of Documents And For Sanctions, (Per Ost Order Filed 6-18-19)

Plantiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant' Compliance With The Court'S May 10, 2019 Orders And For Sanctions. (Per Ost Order Filed On 6-18-19)
Off Calendar Jul-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JENNIFER ZARICH, ESQ. of Law Offices of Linda Ross (Tel: 415-563-2400) appearing for plaintiff Edward A. Byrd Jr.;
Atty. ERIC D. DESPOTES, ESQ. of Clapp Moroney Bucinich Beeman Scheley (Tel: 925-734-0990) appearing for defendants Turner Corporation and Joel Kellogg.

Hearing Re: Plantiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant' Compliance With The Court'S May 10, 2019 Orders And For Sanctions. (Per Ost Order Filed On 6-18-19).

Both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion to compel is DENIED. While the discovery responses ordered by the May 10, 2019, order were originally unverified, which may be treated as non-responsive, verifications were provided and I decline to exercise my discretion to sanction the delay. The sanctions ordered in the May 10 Order were conditional: if Defendant provided the ordered discovery by the time ordered, the sanctions would not be imposed, if not, the sanctions were due. This was intended to incentivize timely compliance. Other than with respect to the verification issue discussed above, Defendants complied with the order, and I conclude this was substantially compliant such that the sanctions were not triggered. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Form Interrogatories,Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jennifer Zarich, esq. (415-563-2400) of Law Offices of Linda Ross for Plaintiff EDWARD BYRD.
No other appearances.

Not argued. Counsel presents proposed order. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EDWARD BYRD's Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Form Interrogatories,Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions:

The motion to compel verified responses is GRANTED as unopposed. Defendants shall provide verifications no later than May 17, 2019. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $60 to reimburse Plaintiff the filing fee. Defendants argue that sanctions are inappropriate under CCP 2030.300(d) because Defendants did not oppose the motion and therefore did not "unsuccessfully ' oppose[] a motion to compel." While this is true for purposes of CCP 2030.300(d), failing to provide the verifications in a timely manner, which required Plaintiff to file this motion, was a failure to confer for purposes of CCP 2023.020. Such failure to meet and confer is a "separate offense entitling the moving party to monetary sanctions 'notwithstanding the outcome of the particular discovery motion.'" Parker v. Wolters Kluwer United States Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 285 (citing CCP 2023.020). Moreover, as cited in Plaintiff's reply, CRC 3.1348(a) permits the court to award sanctions even if discovery is provided or there is no opposition to the motion.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
